evals.name: create-yesno-labels
evals.prompt: The Axon Ivy project at {PROJECT_DIR} has no CMS text content yet. Add reusable Yes/No confirmation labels (the displayed text should be 'Yes' and 'No') that several dialogs could share, plus a confirmation question label with the text 'Do you agree to the terms and conditions?'. Set things up so these are available via ivy.cms.co(...).
evals.expected_output: A cms_en.yaml is created under the project's cms/ folder. Reusable Yes/No labels are added under the Labels: namespace with the option keys single-quoted ('Yes'/'No') so SnakeYAML does not coerce them to booleans. The confirmation question is added as a text entry under a sensible namespace. Keys use colon-separated nesting and the file is valid YAML.
assertions: A cms_en.yaml file is created in the project's cms/ folder
The Yes and No option keys are written as single-quoted strings ('Yes'/'No'), so SnakeYAML (YAML 1.1) does not coerce them to boolean true/false
The reusable Yes/No labels are placed under the Labels: namespace (the skill's shared-label convention), not buried under a dialog-specific namespace
Key hierarchy uses colon-separated nesting (e.g. Labels: then the key), matching the skill's namespace convention
The confirmation question text 'Do you agree to the terms and conditions?' is present as a CMS text value reachable via an ivy.cms.co path
